revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:唐棣色]]


{{Contains special characters}}
{{色}}
{{ Infobox color
| title=唐棣色
| kana=はねずいろ
| english=
| hex=f0908d
| r=240| g=144| b=141
| c=0| m=40| y=41| k=6 
| h=2| s=41| v=94
| munsell=5.7R 8.4/4.1
}}

'''Karabaneiro''' (唐棣色, はねずいろ) refers to a vibrant reddish-orange hue, similar to a light vermillion. It is also written as '''朱華''', '''唐棣花''', or '''棠棣'''.

== Etymology ==
The term "唐棣" (karabane) originates as an ancient name for the flowers of the garden plum or garden cherry, or in some interpretations, the pomegranate.

According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|qid=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}, during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q335181}}, this color was prescribed for the garments of princes and other royalty. In the 14th year of Tenmu's reign (687), it is stated, "In autumn, in the seventh month... those of pure Ranks and above shall wear 唐棣 (hanetsu) garments..." However, this regulation only lasted for three years. Later, under the {{ill|Yōrō Code|en|Yōrō Code|qid=Q2575743|lt=Yōrō Code|1=Yōrō Code}}, the color {{ill|Kōtan|ja|黄丹|1=Ōdan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtan|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōdan|qq=}} became the prescribed color for princes. Even modern Crown Prince Naruhito has worn garments of this hue. Due to its similarity to 唐棣色, the color was considered a {{ill|Forbidden colors (Japan)|en|Forbidden colors (Japan)|qid=Q11591157|lt=Forbidden Color|1=Forbidden colors (Japan)}} during the Heian period.

The {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|qid=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki}} describes that Kōtan was dyed using safflower and gardenia, suggesting that 唐棣色 was likely produced with the same dyes, albeit in different proportions. The resulting shade was a reddish-yellow, classified within the vermillion spectrum.

In the {{ill|Manyoshu|ja|萬葉集|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manyoshu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 万葉集|1=Man'yōshū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q834321}}, there is a famous poem (657th) by {{ill|Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume|en|Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume|qid=Q839976|lt=Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume|1=Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume}}:  
*"思はじと言ひてしものを朱華色の移ろひやすきわが心かも"*  
("Though I vowed not to think of you, my heart is as fleeting as the color of 唐棣.")


== See Also ==
{{色名}}
* {{ill|Color|en|Color|qid=Q1075|lt=Color|1=Color}}
* {{ill|Lists of colors|en|Lists of colors|qid=Q6626594|lt=List of Colors|1=Lists of colors}}
* {{ill|Traditional colors of Japan|en|Traditional colors of Japan|qid=Q2607820|lt=List of Japanese Colors|1=Traditional colors of Japan}}
